items.title: Ebola outbreak kills dozens more as WHO raises alarm over epidemic’s ‘scale and speed’
items.summary: The death toll has risen sharply with hundreds more contracting the virus.

items.title: Carlton quietly settle with former list boss Nick Austin after five-minute ‘blindside’
items.summary: The Blues bounced back on the field and now they’ve made another move after what wasn’t ‘a great week’ for the club.

items.title: Writings of teen shooters reveal motivations for deadly San Diego Mosque attack
items.summary: The ‘radicalised’ pair fatally shot three people before taking their own lives.

items.title: 2026 Toyota GR Yaris: Rally-bred hot hatch gets more performance parts
items.summary: Toyota is giving its GR Yaris hot hatch a new sporty steering wheel, revised power steering, and sticky Bridgestone rubber as part of a small update.

items.title: Prime Minister says EVs could be made in Australia
items.summary: Anthony Albanese has said there’s no reason why electric vehicles can’t be manufactured in Australia.

items.title: NRL announces Wests Tigers, Parramatta, Gold Coast and Melbourne for Las Vegas 2027
items.summary: Three of the four clubs involved currently sit 13th, 15th and 16th on the ladder.

items.title: Arsenal crowned EPL champions after Man City fall short against Bournemouth
items.summary: Pep Guardiola’s reign is ending without one last league trophy.

items.title: Almost 5000 new BYDs and Denzas set sail to Australia to meet demand
items.summary: BYD has chartered one of its own car-carrying ships to transport nearly 5000 new vehicles Down Under to satisfy strong demand for its EVs and PHEVs.

items.title: Trump threatens Iran with strikes while demanding nuclear deal
items.summary: The US President has issued a chilling warning to Iran, threatening another ‘big hit’ while claiming they’re begging to negotiate. But will he follow through?

items.title: Ferrari HC25: Non-hybrid V8 given one more hurrah

items.title: Carlos Alcaraz wiped out of another grand slam as world No.2 withdraws from Wimbledon
items.summary: The world No.2 ‘will be missed’ as the tennis world waits to see if anyone else can challenge Jannik Sinner.
